Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Transcript: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs Air Force Gen. Dan Caine Hold a Press Briefing DoD 31 Mar 2026
  • Strikes Hit Isfahan As US Reportedly Targets Ammunition Depot RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- The United States and Israel reportedly carried out strikes on multiple cities in Isfahan Province overnight on March 30-31, alongside attacks on Tehran.
  • CENTCOM Refutes Media Claims of U.S. Strikes in Lamerd, Iran CENTCOM 31 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Central Command Spokesperson U.S. Navy Capt. Tim Hawkins provided the following statement:
  • Hegseth Impressed With Centcom Troops, 'Wartime Speed' During Epic Fury DoD News 31 Mar 2026 -- A month ago, the U.S. launched Operation Epic Fury, a combat operation laser-focused on destroying Iranian offensive missiles, missile production capability and navy; and ensuring Iran will never have nuclear weapons.
  • US unable to maintain prolonged conflict due to munitions shortages: Analysis Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- A leading American think tank says US weapons stockpiles are being rapidly depleted amid the ongoing aggression against Iran, raising concerns about the United States' ability to sustain a prolonged high-intensity conflict.

  • Iran Is Firing Fewer Missiles. But Its Hit Rate Is Increasing. Why? RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- On the opening day of the war, Iran fired hundreds of ballistic missiles in massive salvos aimed at overwhelming US and Israeli air defenses through sheer numbers. Most were intercepted.
  • Iran Said It Opened A Drone Factory In Tajikistan. But There's No Sign Of It. RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- In May 2022, the head of Iran's armed forces posed for photographers alongside the Tajik defense minister at an undisclosed location near Dushanbe, at a ceremony to announce a major new project.
  • True Promise 4: Iran and resistance axis ops. against US-Israeli assets on Mar. 31 Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- Iranian armed forces and resistance groups across the region continue to carry out retaliatory military operations against the United States and the Israeli regime.
  • Iran's Army targets strategic industrial and tech centers in Tel Aviv, Haifa Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian Army has announced a new wave of drone operations targeting strategic industrial and communication centers linked to Israel.
  • IRGC warns it will target US tech companies for abetting terror in Iran Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has warned 18 major US companies, including tech giants, that their offices and properties in the region will come under attack as they have assisted US-Israeli terror operations inside Iran by providing espionage services.
  • Iran, resistance front pursue long-term strategy to wear down US-Israeli capabilities: IRGC Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- In a series of combined operations, the Iranian armed forces and regional resistance fighters have struck American and Israeli targets as part of the 88th wave of Operation True Promise 4.
  • Iran's air defenses shoot down two more LUCAS drones, one missile Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says Iran's advanced air defense systems have shot down two Low-cost Uncrewed Combat Attack System (LUCAS) drones and a cruise missile over the country's sky.
  • US violations of intl. law deepen global crises, particularly in Strait of Hormuz: IRGC commander Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- A senior Iranian military commander says the United States has failed to achieve its stated objectives in the war against Iran, while warning that violations of international law are deepening global crises, particularly in the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz.
  • Iran air defenses shoot down MQ-9 drone in Isfahan, LUCAS drone over Qeshm Island Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Army says its air defense forces have shot down an American MQ-9 unmanned strategic aircraft over the country's central city of Isfahan and a LUCAS drone over Qeshm Island in the Persian Gulf.
  • US-Israeli aggression on Iran: What happened on 31st day of the imposed war Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- On Monday, the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) announced the martyrdom of Navy Commander Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri, who succumbed to injuries sustained during operations that inflicted heavy damage on enemy facilities and led to the downing of an American fighter jet.
  • Op. True Promise 4, Wave 88: IRGC Navy says it launched 'heavy' attacks on US-Israeli targets Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy has carried out four operations against American and Israeli terrorist targets in the early hours of Tuesday, delivering blows as part of the 88th wave of Operation True Promise 4.

  • Trump to Allies Refusing to Back Iran Op: Fight for Your Own Oil—US Won't Protect You Sputnik 31 Mar 2026 -- The United States will no longer provide assistance to protect the interests of those countries that refused to support the military operation against Iran, US President Donald Trump said on Tuesday.
  • Rubio Says Iran Is At Its 'Weakest Point' RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- In two separate interviews with ABC News and Al Jazeera, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that the United States is making rapid progress in degrading Iran's military capabilities, and expects to achieve its objectives "in a matter of weeks, not months," while emphasizing that US President Donald Trump "prefers diplomacy."
  • Trump tells aides he's willing to end Iran war even if Strait of Hormuz stays shut: Report Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 31 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump told aides he is prepared to wind down the US military campaign against Iran even if the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed, Anadolu Agency reported coting the Wall Street Journal (WSJ).
  • Trump Says US Operation Against Iran Will Not Last Too Much Longer Sputnik 31 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Tuesday that the military operation against Iran will not last too much longer.
  • In major backdown, Trump seeks to end aggression on Iran without reopening Hormuz: Report Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- President Donald Trump is reportedly willing to end the US's unprovoked aggression against Iran without reopening the strategic Strait of Hormuz in a move seen by observers as a major backdown.

  • Statement by Prime Minister Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 31 Mar 2026 -- "Marking one month of our joint campaign with the US, we are systematically crushing the terrorist regime that for decades cried: 'Death to America, Death to Israel.'"

  • Araqchi Says Iran Has Not Responded To US Plan Or Presented Any Conditions RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said that he continues to receive indirect messages from US special envoy Steve Witkoff but that does not mean "negotiations" are taking place between Tehran and Washington.
  • Iranian President Says Tehran Has 'Necessary Will' To End War But Seeks Security Guarantees RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- Iranian President Masud Pezeshkian says his country has the "necessary will" to end the war with the United States and Israel depending on conditions, mainly that his country's security is guaranteed.
  • IRGC Confirms General Eshaqi Killed In US-Israeli Strikes RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) confirmed on March 31 that IRGC Major General Jamshid Eshaqi, adviser to the Chief of Staff of the Armed Forces, had been killed along with several members of his family in US and Israeli strikes.
  • Iran ready to end war if aggressors commit to firm guarantees: Pezeshkian Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian says the country is ready to end the ongoing fight against the US-Israeli aggression if it receives firm guarantees that it will not be attacked again in the future.
  • Iran warns of new false-flag attacks, denies reports of missile strike on Turkey Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has warned about an imminent wave of false-flag operations orchestrated by enemies attempting to drag the region into a wider conflict.
  • Iran intelligence forces arrest 54 mercenaries linked to US, Israel across four provinces Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Intelligence Ministry has announced the arrest of 54 operatives working for the American-Israeli enemy across four provinces, dealing a blow to espionage networks and terrorist cells operating inside the country.
  • Two MKO terrorists executed for attacks on sensitive sites in Tehran Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- Two armed terrorists affiliated with the Mujahedin-e-Khalq (MKO) group have been executed by hanging after being convicted of multiple terrorist operations targeting state institutions and public places in the Iranian capital, the Iranian Judiciary has announced.
  • Pezeshkian Says Iran Has 'Necessary Will' For Peace, Buoying Hopes Deal May Be Nearing RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- Iranian President Masud Pezeshkian says his country has the "necessary will" to end the war with the United States and Israel depending on conditions, a possible sign the countries may be moving toward settling the conflict.
  • Parliament speaker rejects Trump's reckless ultimatums, says any strike on Iran will trigger decisive retaliation Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf has denounced US President Donald Trump's aggressive threats against the Islamic Republic, warning that any act of aggression will be met with a crushing response from the Iranian nation.

  • 'No precedent' for seafarers caught in war zone in post-WW2 era UN News 31 Mar 2026 -- Some 20,000 seafarers remain stranded on ships in the Strait of Hormuz as the war in the Middle East continues, a situation which has been described as unprecedented in the post-Second World War era.
  • Global disruptions to supply chains are driving tomorrow's hunger crisis WFP 31 Mar 2026 -- This is a transcript of what was said by Corinne Fleischer, WFP Director of Supply Chain (speaking from Rome via Zoom) - to whom quoted text may be attributed - at today's press briefing at the Palais des Nations in Geneva.
  • Humanitarian crisis for children deepens after one month of war in the Middle East UNICEF 31 Mar 2026 -- Beyond the staggering casualty toll, delays and disruptions in supply operations put children at risk across the globe

  • China Slams Strikes on Iran's Peaceful Nuclear Facilities Sputnik 31 Mar 2026 -- Strikes on Iran's peaceful nuclear facilities could have serious consequences for regional security and stability, and China resolutely opposes them, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Mao Ning stated at a briefing on Tuesday.
  • China, Pakistan put forward five-point proposals for situation in Gulf and Middle East, calling for immediately ceasing hostilities Global Times 31 Mar 2026 -- China and Pakistan put forward five proposals on restoring peace and stability in the Gulf region and the Middle East during talks between Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi and Pakistani Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar in Beijing on Tuesday.
  • Five-Point Initiative of China and Pakistan For Restoring Peace and Stability in the Gulf and Middle East Region (Beijing, 31 March 2026) Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 31 Mar 2026 -- Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and the Minister of Foreign Affairs of the People's Republic of China H.E. Mr. Wang Yi and Deputy Prime Minister/Foreign Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, Senator Mohammad Ishaq Dar met in Beijing on 31 March 2026 to review the situation in the Gulf and Middle East Region.
  • China, Pakistan Coordinate On Iran Talks As War Disrupts Global Trade RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- China and Pakistan vowed to "strengthen strategic communication and coordination" as they seek to help broker a deal to end the war in Iran, now in its fifth week with no clear end in sight.
  • China confirms three vessels have navigated through Hormuz Strait in recent days Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 31 Mar 2026 -- Three Chinese vessels have navigated through the Strait of Hormuz recently with the facilitation and coordination of relevant sides, Xinhua reports citing Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson Mao Ning.
  • Three Chinese ships recently transited Strait of Hormuz; China expresses appreciation for relevant parties' assistance: FM Global Times 31 Mar 2026 -- After coordination with relevant parties, three Chinese ships recently transited the Strait of Hormuz, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Tuesday. The remarks were made in response to a question on whether the Foreign Ministry has received any further assurance from Iran guaranteeing shipping safety or the status of traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, after tracking data from a monitoring group has shown two container vessels belonging to COSCO have successfully passed through the Strait of Hormuz and have exited the Gulf.

  • Russian FM: US, Israel seeking to pit Arab states against Iran Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- Russian Minister of Foreign Affairs Sergei Lavrov says the United States and Israel are seeking to pit the Arab states of the Persian Gulf region against Iran amid the US-Israeli military aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • Putin, UAE Leader Express Concern About Deteriorating Situation in Middle East - Kremlin Sputnik 31 Mar 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin held a phone call with his UAE counterpart, Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, during which they expressed serious concern about the degradation of the military-political situation in the Middle East and the death of people, the Kremlin said on Tuesday.

  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Two Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in the Past Hours SPA 31 Mar 2026
  • Civil Defense Reports Limited Material Damage to Six Houses in Al-Kharj Following Drone Interception; No Injuries SPA 31 Mar 2026 -- The Civil Defense stated that on Tuesday, March 31, debris from an intercepted drone fell on six houses in a neighborhood in Al-Kharj Governorate, causing limited material damage, with no injuries reported
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Four Ballistic Missiles Launched toward Riyadh Region Intercepted and Destroyed SPA 31 Mar 2026

  • France, Italy join Spain in resisting Trump's pressure to join war on Iran Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- France and Italy have joined Spain in resisting Donald Trump's pressure to support US-Israeli aggression against Iran, as the illegal war he launched is now in its fifth week.
  • PH to engage Iran to ensure oil shipment passage in Strait of Hormuz Philippine News Agency 31 Mar 2026 -- The Philippine government will initiate diplomatic talks with Iran to help ensure the safe passage of its fuel shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil transit route, Malacañang said Tuesday.
  • 'We'll break down borders': Iraqi resistance warns US against using Kuwaiti territory to strike Iran Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- An Iraqi anti-terror resistance group issues the United States a strong warning against its using the Kuwaiti territory for strikes against Iran.
  • Poland Refuses to Hand Over Patriot Systems to US - Defense Minister Sputnik 31 Mar 2026 -- Poland does not intend to provide the US with its Patriot missile defense systems, Minister of National Defense of the Republic Wladyslaw Kosiniak-Kamysz stated.
  • Gulf air defences boosted further, as Defence Secretary visits Middle East UK MOD 31 Mar 2026 -- Further vital air defence support for Gulf partners has been confirmed by Defence Secretary John Healey MP as he visits the Middle East.

  • New Executions A Sign Of Growing Repression In Iran Amid War RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- Iran announced the execution of two political prisoners on March 31, a day after the death penalty was also carried out on two others, in a sign that the clerical authorities are stepping up a crackdown on internal opposition in the country.

  • Deploying Forces To Middle East 'Sends Message' To Iran, Says Ex-US General RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- Iran is using tactical delay as a strategy in talks to end US and Israeli strikes that have decimated Tehran's military capabilities amid a continued buildup of US forces in the Middle East, retired US Army General Joseph L. Votel -- who led US Central Command from 2016 to 2019 -- told RFE/RL in an interview on March 30.

  • Iran-US Contacts Likely Ongoing Despite Public Denials, Says Analyst RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- Speaking to Current Time, political analyst Anton Penkovsky said contacts between Iran and the United States are likely continuing despite public denials by Tehran.

  • Energy Markets Could Take Months To Stabilize After War Ends, Expert Says RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- Even if hostilities with Iran were to stop immediately, global energy markets will take a long time to recover, Mark P. Mills, executive director of the National Center for Energy Analytics, told RFE/RL.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Chairman Commends Military Industrial Base Workers DoD News 31 Mar 2026 -- The military industrial base workers produce the weapons, platforms and systems that allow the warfighters to succeed on the battlefield, said Air Force Gen. Dan Caine, chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, who spoke at a Pentagon press briefing today.
  • Sixth iteration of Power Wargame provides strategic insights for leaders USSTRATCOM 31 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Strategic Command recently brought together experts in the U.S. Government and from Allied nations to use rigor and data to consider and examine ways to deescalate an international crisis.
  • Coast Guard, partner agencies to support historic Artemis II launch US Coast Guard 31 Mar 2026 -- Coast Guard and partner agency crews are scheduled to support NASA's Artemis II launch beginning Wednesday at the Kennedy Space Center in Cape Canaveral.
  • Submarine Rescue Diving and Recompression System Completes Certification US Navy 31 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Navy certified the Submarine Rescue Diving and Recompression System (SRDRS) for full operation, March 23, clearing the system for deployment in support of undersea rescue missions around the globe.
  • U.S. Strengthens Maritime Security Cooperation with Kenya During NAVAF Commander's Visit US Navy 31 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Naval Forces Africa (NAVAF) strengthened its longstanding security partnership with Kenya during Adm. George Wikoff's visit to Nairobi and Mombasa, March 24-25.
  • Massachusetts Guard, Kenya strengthen partnership at African Land Forces Summit Army News 31 Mar 2026 -- Senior leaders from the Massachusetts National Guard attended the African Land Forces Summit in Rome, Italy, March 23-24, alongside senior leaders of the Kenya Defence Forces, marking more than a decade of partnership through the State Partnership Program.
  • Nevada Guard Soldiers strengthen ROK-US alliance during Freedom Shield 2026 Army News 31 Mar 2026 -- Soldiers from the Nevada Army National Guard's 17th Sustainment Brigade participated in Exercise Freedom Shield 2026, a multinational command post exercise held March 9-19, marking the unit's first involvement alongside the Republic of Korea Army, U.S. Forces Korea and United Nations Command.
  • Nebraska National Guard and Tanzania formalize State Partnership Army News 31 Mar 2026 -- In a move that significantly expands U.S. security cooperation in East Africa, military leaders from the Nebraska National Guard and the Tanzania Peoples' Defence Forces officially formalized their partnership on March 12.

  • Multinational MRTT Unit achieves Full Operational Capability (FOC) Czech Republic Ministry of Defence & Armed Forces 31 Mar 2026 -- The Multinational MRTT Unit (MMU) has officially achieved Full Operational Capability (FOC). This milestone concludes a journey that began with Initial Operational Capability (IOC) in 2023. What started as an ambitious multinational initiative has matured into a fully operational and trusted capability. The event also featured a change of command: Colonel Evert Cuppens (BEL) took over the MMU as the new Commander from Colonel Ludger Bette (DEU), who was given a well-deserved send-off into retirement.

  • If EU States Give Ukraine Airspace to Try Attack, Russia Will Take Measures - Kremlin Sputnik 31 Mar 2026 -- If EU countries really give Ukraine airspace to try to attack Russia, Russia will be obliged to take appropriate measures, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Tuesday.

  • Ukraine and Bulgaria expand cooperation in drones and ammunition production Ukraine MOD 31 Mar 2026 -- Ukraine and Bulgaria will further strengthen cooperation in developing unmanned technologies, countering drones, and manufacturing ammunition to meet the needs of the Defence Forces of Ukraine.
  • One of the most effective interceptors against Shahed drones: what is known about the Ukrainian-made Shvidun drone Ukraine MOD 31 Mar 2026 -- The Ministry of Defence has codified and authorized the Ukrainian-made Shvidun unmanned aerial system (UAS) for operational use within the Armed Forces of Ukraine.

  • Unmanned air truck takes flight: China's 7-tonne cargo drone completes maiden flight Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 31 Mar 2026 -- China's first 7-tonne-class large transport drone, Changying-8, completed its maiden flight on Tuesday at the Zhengzhou Shangjie Airport in central China's Henan Province, becoming the largest cargo drone in the world to have flown, according to CGTN.
  • China's first 7-ton fully indigenous cargo drone NORINCO LUCA makes successful maiden flight, expanding low-altitude logistics capabilities Global Times 31 Mar 2026 -- The fully domestically developed NORINCO LUCA large cargo unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) successfully completed its maiden flight on Tuesday in Zhengzhou, Central China's Henan Province. According to its developer from China's state-owned Norinco Group, it is now the largest logistics unmanned cargo aircraft in the world to have achieved a successful first flight, marking the arrival of China's first 7-ton-class fixed-wing "unmanned aerial heavy truck" in the low-altitude economy and expanding the range of potential applications.

  • Vice Minister Chung Inspects Joint Military-Public-Civilian-Medical Integration Exercise in Southern Region, Commends Personnel and Encourages Advancement of Medical Capabilities ROC MND 31 Mar 2026
  • Vice Minister Hsu Continues to Manage Military Procurement Progress and Builds Solid Defensive Combat Power ROC MND 31 Mar 2026

  • Defence estate community consultation sessions begin Australia DOD 31 Mar 2026 -- Community consultation sessions will begin in Tasmania next week as part of the Albanese Government's nationwide response to the independent Defence Estate Audit.
  • Chief of the Defence Force Keynote Address to the ANU National Security College Conference Australia DOD 31 Mar 2026

  • LAUNCH OF FIRST NEXT GENERATION OFFSHORE PATROL VESSEL (YARD 1280, SHACHI) AT M/S GSL, GOA India PIB 31 Mar 2026 -- Yard 1280 (Shachi), the first of eleven Next Generation Offshore Patrol Vessels (NGOPVs), was launched at M/s GSL, Goa on 31 Mar 2026 by Mrs Shagun Sobti, in the presence of VAdm Tarun Sobti, Deputy Chief of the Naval Staff.
  • DELIVERY OF MALWAN - THE SECOND ANTI-SUBMARINE WARFARE SHALLOW WATER CRAFT BUILT BY CSL, KOCHI India PIB 31 Mar 2026 -- 'Malwan', the second of eight Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft (ASW SWC) built by Cochin Shipyard Limited (CSL), Kochi, was delivered to the Indian Navy on 31 Mar 2026.
  • Aatmanirbhar Bharat: MoD & BEL sign Rs 1,950 crore contract for Mountain Radars for IAF India PIB 31 Mar 2026 -- Strengthening India's indigenous defence capabilities under Aatmanirbhar Bharat and Make-in-India, Ministry of Defence (MoD) has inked a major capital acquisition contract with Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) for the procurement of two Mountain Radars, including associated equipment and required infrastructure for the Indian Air Force, at a cost of around Rs 1,950 crore. The contract, under Buy (Indian-Indigenously Designed Developed and Manufactured) category, was signed in the presence of senior officials of MoD & BEL in New Delhi on March 31, 2026.
  • INDIAN NAVAL SHIP TRIKAND CONCLUDES PORT CALL AT MAPUTO, MOZAMBIQUE India PIB 31 Mar 2026 -- INS Trikand, a frontline guided missile frigate of the Indian Navy, concluded her port call at Maputo, Mozambique on 29 Mar 2026.
  • FOURTH SURVEY VESSEL (LARGE) 'SANSHODHAK' DELIVERED TO INDIAN NAVY AT GRSE KOLKATA India PIB 31 Mar 2026 -- Sanshodhak (Yard 3028), the last Survey Vessel (Large) ship steered by the Indian Navy's Warship Design Bureau, being built at Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers Ltd (GRSE), Kolkata was delivered to the Indian Navy on 30 Mar 2026.
  • INDIGENOUS STEALTH FRIGATE 'DUNAGIRI' DELIVERED TO INDIAN NAVY - A MAJOR BOOST TO AATMANIRBHARTA IN WARSHIP BUILDING India PIB 31 Mar 2026 -- Dunagiri (Yard 3023), the fifth ship of Nilgiri Class (Project 17A) and second of the class built at Garden Reach Shipbuilding and Engineers Ltd (GRSE), was delivered to the Indian Navy on 30 Mar 2026 at GRSE, Kolkata, marking a major milestone in achieving self-reliance in warship design and construction. Project 17A frigates are versatile multi-mission platforms, designed to address current and future challenges in the maritime domain.

  • Potential new air threat at East- and West- Virumaa and South Estonia Estonia Defence Forces 31 Mar 2026 -- As a result of the Russian Federation's aggression against Ukraine, potentially dangerous aerial vehicles may enter Estonian airspace. The Defence Forces have identified activity outside Estonian airspace that may pose a threat to Estonia. Therefore, a preventive threat alert has been issued. The threat will be announced separately on threat alert channels and state channels. It is recommended to stay indoors in the listed areas.

  • EU-Egypt Security and Defence Dialogue European External Action Service (EEAS) 31 Mar 2026 -- On 31 March 2026, the 1st EU-Egypt Security and Defence Dialogue was held in Cairo.

  • UK and Belgium sign multi-million pound Mission Data partnership UK MOD 31 Mar 2026 -- The UK and Belgium have signed an MOU that will see Belgium invest over five years in UK expertise to develop world-class mission data collection and exploitation.

  • The air threat in Estonia has ended Estonia Defence Forces 31 Mar 2026 -- On the night of March 31, as a result of the Russian Federation's aggression against Ukraine, an air threat existed in certain areas of Estonia, during which potentially dangerous aerial vehicles could have entered Estonian airspace.

  • Arctic Security Forces Roundtable convenes in Iceland Government of Iceland 31 Mar 2026 -- Iceland hosted the Arctic Security Forces Roundtable (ASFR) in Keflavík on 24-25 March, bringing together senior military leaders to discuss developments in security and defence across the Arctic region. The meeting focused on strengthening shared situational awareness and enhancing coordination among Allies in the Arctic.

  • Responsibility for security of the Baltic skies transferred to the French and Romanian Air Forces Lithuania MND 31 Mar 2026 -- On March 31, Air Contingents of the French and the Romanian Air Forces took over the responsibility for the NATO Air Policing Mission in the Baltic states at a ceremony in Šiauliai. They replace the Spanish Air Force Contingent after eight months of duty.

  • Theon Sensors Belgium Subsidiary Inaugurated OCCAR 31 Mar 2026 -- The OCCAR-EA participated in the inauguration ceremony of Theon Sensors Belgium. The founding of the subsidiary in Belgium is part of the Infrared Clip-on Device (IRCOD) Contract which was signed on 24 September 2025. OCCAR-EA in line with the procurement procedure, secured requirements regarding a Belgian industry workshare and a transfer of technology.

  • The Romanian Air Force - a new Enhanced Air Policing Mission in the Baltic airspace Romania - Ministry of National Defence 31 Mar 2026 -- The Romanian Air Force will begin a new Enhanced Air Policing Mission in the Baltic airspace on Wednesday, April 1st, as part of Romania's commitment to NATO's collective security.
  • Minister of National Defence at Romania Government Roundtable "South-East Europe's Next Leap Forward" Romania - Ministry of National Defence 31 Mar 2026 -- Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, participated on Tuesday, March 31, in the Romania Government Roundtable "South-East Europe's Next Leap Forward", organized under the aegis of The Economist, a format dedicated to analyzing the role of South-East Europe in the context of current global transformations.

  • Visiting forces accord with France backs PH on rules-based int'l order Philippine News Agency 31 Mar 2026 -- A ranking Philippine Navy (PN) official on Tuesday said the just signed Status of Visiting Forces Agreement (SOVFA) with France is a clear indication that the Philippines' stand for rules-based international order is supported by other nations in the world.
  • 8K PH, allied troops to join 'Salaknib' drills in Luzon Philippine News Agency 31 Mar 2026 -- More than 8,000 soldiers from the Philippines, the United States, Australia, Japan, and New Zealand will join another iteration of the "Salaknib" drills that will start on April 6 and take place in northern and central Luzon for the next two weeks.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Portuguese Foreign Minister Rangel US Dept. of State 31 Mar 2026
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio With Sean Hannity of Fox News US Dept. of State 31 Mar 2026

Defense Industry

  • Contracts for March 31, 2026 DoD
  • Airbus tests low-cost 'Bird of Prey' autonomous interceptor to hunt kamikaze drones Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 31 Mar 2026 -- Airbus has advanced its counter-drone efforts with a successful first flight of its new "Bird of Prey" interceptor, according to Interesting Engineering.

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 31 March 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 31 Mar 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • 'Discriminatory by design': Israeli regime approves death penalty bill against Palestinians Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- The Israeli parliament has passed a discriminatory bill that targets Palestinians with death penalty, a new means of killing more Palestinians by the occupying entity, which the Palestinian Authority has already condemned as a "war crime."
  • Israel Passes Death Penalty Law for Terrorists - National Security Minister Sputnik 31 Mar 2026 -- The Israeli Parliament passed a law on the death penalty for terrorists, Minister of National Security Itamar Ben-Gvir said on Monday.

  • Four Israeli troops killed, two wounded in Hezbollah operations Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- At least four Israeli troops have been killed in southern Lebanon as fighters from the Lebanese resistance movement Hezbollah carried out waves of operations against the occupation forces in defense of Lebanon and its nation.

  • UN Relief Chief tells Security Council: "We cannot let Lebanon fail" UNOCHA 31 Mar 2026 -- Briefing to the Security Council on the humanitarian situation in Lebanon by Tom Fletcher, Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator
  • Officials Warn of Escalating Crisis in Lebanon, as Security Council Speakers Trade Blame for Death of Three Peacekeepers, Urge Respect for Ceasefire UN Security Council 31 Mar 2026 -- Destruction, displacement and widespread panic have returned to Lebanon, senior United Nations officials in the region detailed today, as the Security Council convened an emergency meeting following the deaths of three peacekeepers and delegates traded accusations over who was responsible.
  • Lebanon at 'breaking point' as displacement soars and strikes intensify UN News 31 Mar 2026 -- The UN's top humanitarian official warned the Security Council on Tuesday that Lebanon is facing one of its most dangerous moments in years, with escalating violence, mass displacement and deepening human suffering pushing the country to "breaking point".
  • Lebanon: The UN peacekeepers helping communities who've stayed behind UN News 31 Mar 2026 -- The killing of three UN peacekeepers in south Lebanon in the past two days has highlighted the grave dangers for the 10,000 military personnel who make up the bulk of UNIFIL, the UN Interim Force in Lebanon - and for the communities caught up in intense clashes between the Israeli military and Hezbollah fighters.
  • UN condemns killing of two more peacekeepers in Lebanon UN News 31 Mar 2026 -- The United Nations has condemned two consecutive days of deadly attacks on peacekeepers serving with the UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL), amid rising hostilities between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants.
  • Israel: Türk warns against legislative steps targeting Palestinians in violation of international law OHCHR 31 Mar 2026 -- UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk on Tuesday urged Israel to repeal a law passed by its parliament on 30 March reinstating the death penalty, in terms applicable almost exclusively to Palestinians.
  • UNHCR: Over 200,000 people cross into Syria after a month of hostilities in Lebanon UNHCR 31 Mar 2026 -- One month after hostilities intensified in the region and in Lebanon, Syria has seen a sharp rise in people crossing the border from Lebanon. Between 2 and 27 March, more than 200,000 people entered Syria through the three official crossing points, according to Syrian authorities.

  • 'War crime': Outrage after Israel passes death penalty law for Palestinian prisoners Press TV 31 Mar 2026 -- Human rights groups and Palestinian leaders have strongly denounced Israel's new legislation approving the death penalty for Palestinian prisoners.
  • PACE President: Knesset vote 'seriously jeopardises' Israel's observer status with the Assembly PACE 31 Mar 2026
  • MOFA Spokesperson's Statement Regarding Recent Casualties Sustained by Indonesian Peacekeepers during their Service in UNIFIL Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Republic of Korea 31 Mar 2026
  • Statement condemning continued measures and restrictions imposed by Israeli authorities on freedom of worship in Jerusalem city League of Arab States 31 Mar 2026
  • General Secretariat statement condemning Israeli Knesset's approval of law on executing Palestinian prisoners League of Arab States 31 Mar 2026
  • The OIC Condemns the Approval of the Law on the Execution of Palestinian Prisoners Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 31 Mar 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has expressed its strong condemnation of the approval by the so-called Israeli Knesset of a law permitting the execution of Palestinian prisoners, considering this a dangerous and unprecedented step that grants a licence for murder and political execution against the Palestinian people, in flagrant violation of international humanitarian law, particularly the Fourth Geneva Convention and the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights
  • Response to the approval by the Israeli Parliament of a bill enabling capital punishment Republic of Slovenia 31 Mar 2026
  • Spain condemns passing of law in Israeli Parliament on death penalty in Israel and the State of Palestine Spain MFA 31 Mar 2026
  • Israel: Statement by the High Representative on behalf of the European Union on the approval of the Death Penalty Bill by the Israeli Parliament EC/CEU 31 Mar 2026
  • HE GCCSG Condemns in the Strongest Terms the Israeli Occupation's Decision to Execute Palestinian Prisoners Gulf Cooperation Council 31 Mar 2026
  • No: 59, 31 March 2026, Regarding the Law on the Death Penalty Adopted in the Israeli Parliament Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 31 Mar 2026

  • OIC Welcomes the Adoption of Resolutions on Palestine by the United Nations Human Rights Council Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 31 Mar 2026 -- The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) welcomes the adoption of a number of resolutions on the question of Palestine by the United Nations Human Rights Council during its 61st session, held in Geneva from 23 February 2026 to 31 March 2026
  • The OIC Media Observatory on Israeli Crimes Against Palestinians Issues its 122nd Report as Al-Aqsa, Ibrahimi, and Church of the Holy Sepulchre Remain Closed to Worshippers Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 31 Mar 2026 -- The OIC Media Observatory on Israeli Crimes Against Palestinians recorded the killing and wounding of 138 Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and the West Bank between March 24 and 30, 2026
  • No: 58, 31 March 2026, Regarding the Attacks on the UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 31 Mar 2026
  • Joint Statement on Lebanon UK FCDO 31 Mar 2026 -- Statement by the Foreign Ministers of Belgium, Croatia, Cyprus, France, Greece, Italy, Malta, the Netherlands, Portugal, and the United Kingdom, and the High Representative of the European Union on Lebanon
  • Israel's decision to expand its ground operations further into Lebanon undermines work to disarm Hizballah and threatens Lebanon's sovereignty: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 31 Mar 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Lebanon.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Syria, Sudan, Venezuela UNOCHA 31 Mar 2026

  • 90 Chinese warships, coast guard vessels spotted in WPS in March Philippine News Agency 31 Mar 2026 -- A total of 90 Chinese warships and coast guard vessels were monitored throughout March across four key features in the West Philippine Sea (WPS), a senior naval official reported Tuesday.

  • Afghanistan: UN experts demand immediate end to Taliban restrictions on women at UN premises OHCHR 31 Mar 2026 -- UN experts* today expressed alarm over the continuing ban preventing Afghan women from accessing UN premises, including UN national staff, contractors, and visitors.

  • Syria - an opportunity that must be grasped European External Action Service (EEAS) 31 Mar 2026
  • PM meeting with President Ahmed al-Sharaa of Syria: 31 March 2026 Gov.UK 31 Mar 2026
  • Syria: Hundreds of thousands flee Lebanon, vital food aid blocked UN News 31 Mar 2026 -- The trauma of mass displacement and disruption to aid shipments throughout the world are among the devastating impacts of the war raging in the Middle East, UN agencies said on Tuesday.

  • IOM Moves to Expand Response in Sudan, Appeals for USD 277 Million IOM 31 Mar 2026 -- The International Organization for Migration (IOM) is appealing for USD 277 million to support the most vulnerable people affected by displacement and conflict in Sudan and neighbouring countries in 2026, as the crisis approaches its third year.

News Reports

  • Federal Judge Revokes Naturalization of Married Couple Who Conspired to Steal Medical Trade Secrets and Share Them with China US Dept. of Justice 31 Mar 2026 -- On March 30, Judge James E. Simmons Jr., of the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of California entered an order revoking the naturalized U.S. citizenship of husband and wife Li Chen and Yu Zhou, finding they illegally procured their naturalization.

  • Investigation Into Unmanned Aerial Vehicles That Fell to the Ground Progresses Police of Finland 31 Mar 2026 -- The investigation by authorities into the unmanned aerial vehicles that fell on Sunday, 29th March 2026, is ongoing.

  • Ministry of Foreign Affairs expresses protest to Russia over its disinformation campaign Latvia MFA 31 Mar 2026 -- Today, 31 March 2026, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs submitted an official note of protest to the Russian Embassy in Latvia regarding Russia's disinformation campaign against Latvia.

  • NASA begins the countdown for humanity's first launch to the moon in 53 years Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 31 Mar 2026 -- NASA began the countdown Monday for humanity's first launch to the moon in 53 years, according to the Associated Press.
  • NASA set for first crewed moon return in over half a century Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 31 Mar 2026 -- NASA is preparing to launch the first crew of astronauts toward the moon in over 53 years with its second Artemis mission, a critical test flight in humanity's broader lunar goals as the U.S. races to reassert leadership in space faced with growing competition from China, Reuters reported.

  • Trump-Xi talks could make Taiwan safer: Visiting U.S. senators CNA 31 Mar 2026 -- A visiting United States senator said Tuesday that a planned meeting between President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping is "not necessarily a bad thing for Taiwan," as stronger ties between Washington and Beijing could mean a safer Taipei.
  • READOUT: Ranking Member Shaheen and Senator Curtis's Bipartisan Delegation Travel to Taiwan US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 31 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Senator Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and Senator John Curtis (R-UT) led a bipartisan Congressional delegation to Taiwan for meetings with top government and defense officials, civil society and private sector leaders on March 30 and 31. The delegation also included Senator Thom Tillis (R-NC) and Senator Jacky Rosen (D-NV).
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 31 Mar 2026

  • China's Changying-8 unmanned cargo aircraft completes maiden flight People's Daily 31 Mar 2026 -- Changying-8 cargo unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) successfully completed its maiden flight at an airport in Zhengzhou, central China's Henan Province, on Tuesday.

  • S. Hwanghae Province Strives to Minimize Drought Damage KCNA 31 Mar 2026 -- South Hwanghae Province of the DPRK concentrates all its efforts on minimizing the drought damage to crops.
  • Adoption of Resolution on Situation of Human Rights in DPRK at 61st Session of UN Human Rights Council Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Republic of Korea 31 Mar 2026 -- A resolution on the situation of human rights in the DPRK, co-sponsored by 50 countries including the Republic of Korea, was adopted by consensus at the 61st session of the UN Human Rights Council (UNHRC) on March 30 (Geneva time).

  • UN Human Rights Council 61: UK General Comment on Myanmar UK FCDO 31 Mar 2026 -- UK General Comment on Myanmar. Delivered by UK Human Rights Ambassador, Eleanor Sanders.

  • India - Russia Foreign Office Consultations (March 30, 2026) India MEA 31 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri held Foreign Office Consultations with Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Andrey Rudenko on March 30, 2026.

  • The Daily Press Briefing (03/31/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 31 Mar 2026

  • UN General Assembly adopts landmark resolution to strengthen the work of the UN system UN News 31 Mar 2026 -- The United Nations General Assembly on Tuesday adopted a landmark resolution to strengthen how UN mandates - the decisions taken by Member States that guide the Organisation's work - are created, implemented and reviewed across the system.

  • Activist's Case Highlights Concerns Over Digital Surveillance In Kazakhstan RFE/RL 31 Mar 2026 -- After publicly calling for the release of the Kazakh rights movement Atazhurt, Bagdat Togysbayev was detained for five days by police. But what struck him as odd wasn't being behind bars, it was how his phone worked afterward.

  • PH secures 51-day fuel supply buffer amid Middle East crisis Philippine News Agency 31 Mar 2026 -- The Philippines now has a 51-day fuel supply buffer as the government accelerates efforts to secure oil stocks amid the ongoing Middle East conflict, President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. said Tuesday.
